title: Merge Sort — Count Inversions
description: Given an array of n integers, count the number of inversions: pairs (i,j) with i<j and a[i]>a[j]. Use a merge-sort based approach for O(n log n).
inputSpec: First line: n. Second line: n integers.

botCodes.code:
def merge_count(a):
    if len(a)<=1:return a,0
    mid=len(a)//2
    L,lc=merge_count(a[:mid])
    R,rc=merge_count(a[mid:])
    merged,inv=[],lc+rc
    i=j=0
    while i<len(L) and j<len(R):
        if L[i]<=R[j]:merged.append(L[i]);i+=1
        else:merged.append(R[j]);inv+=len(L)-i;j+=1
    merged+=L[i:]+R[j:]
    return merged,inv
n=int(input())
a=list(map(int,input().split()))
_,c=merge_count(a)
print(c)

let cnt=ref 0
let rec merge_count a=match a with|[]|[_]->a|_->let mid=List.length a/2 in let l=merge_count(List.filteri(fun i _->i<mid) a) and r=merge_count(List.filteri(fun i _->i>=mid) a) in let rec merge l r=match l,r with|[],r->r|l,[]->l|x::lt,y::rt->if x<=y then x::merge lt r else(cnt:= !cnt+List.length l;y::merge l rt) in merge l r
let()=let n=Scanf.scanf" %d" Fun.id in let a=List.init n(fun _->Scanf.scanf" %d" Fun.id) in ignore(merge_count a);Printf.printf"%d\n" !cnt
